title: Analyst, Market Business Partner
description:
Be a part of a revolutionary change

At PMI, we’ve chosen to do something incredible. We’re totally transforming our business and building our future on one clear purpose – to deliver a smoke-free future.

With huge change, comes huge opportunity. So, wherever you join us, you’ll enjoy the freedom to dream up and deliver better, brighter solutions and the space to move your career forward in endlessly different directions.

Being part of the Finance team at PMI feels like building a new business – but with the accumulated expertise of a global brand behind you. Bring us your curiosity and capacity to engage people and you can be part of the Finance team that’s driving our commercial organization forward.

Your ‘day to day’

In this role with Philip Morris Singapore Pte Ltd, you will be responsible for the following:

Financial Planning & Forecasting

• Manage monthly & Financial Year volume forecast to ensure forecast accuracy

• Support monthly Integrated Business Planning, Monthly Business Updates & budget management - coordinate volume, Share of Market and financials target setting

• Monitor key volume drivers across channels and seasonal cycles to enhance forecast accuracy and guide financial decision‑making.

• Strengthen supply planning processes with the supply chain team to ensure accurate volume alignment, mitigate operational and inventory‑related risks, and enhance working capital efficiency.

• Support Commercial budget, Original Budget/Rolling Forecast reporting and commercial budget allocation and deployment

• Provide recommendations, insights, and Return of Investment evaluation on commercial initiatives, Point of Sale Marketing investment, contract outlets, Partner for Performance mechanics/analysis

Commercial Finance/ Scenario Building

• Deliver data‑driven performance evaluations to generate commercially relevant insights.

• Analyze & simulate macro-economic trend to support strategic direction setting & decisions making

• Support scenario modelling by analyzing key volume drivers and their implications for business performance.

Commercial deployment

• Ensure effective utilization of commercial resources to strengthen business performance oversight.

• Define channel & customer segmentation and determine priorities

• Develop and support channel / account strategy

• Support commercial cycle objectives framing and deployment


Who we’re looking for

• Bachelor’s Degree or equivalent experience holder, preferably in Finance, Economics, or Business Administration.

• Minimum of 3 years of related experiences

• Strong business acumen and budgeting & forecasting skills.

• Proficient in financial modeling, analytical, and problem-solving skills.

• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with diverse teams.

• Proactive, results-oriented, and able to work independently as well as part of a team.

postedCompany.description: PHILIP MORRIS SINGAPORE PTE. LTD is an affiliate of Philip Morris International (PMI), the leading international tobacco company, with seven of the world’s top 15 brands, including the number one brand worldwide. PMI has more than 87,000 employees and its products are sold in approximately 180 countries. PMI is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
postedCompany.name: PHILIP MORRIS SINGAPORE PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
